summary: Verified the `uploads/uploads/` double-prefix bug fix in /app/backend/routes/event_tool.py (removed local normalize_image_url shadow inside get_my_order_by_id). Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_image_url_double_prefix_fix.py with 19 cases — 9 unit tests on the global utils.image_helper.normalize_image_url contract + 10 integration tests (smoke health, CORS preflight for farforevent.com.ua, PRIMARY order-detail image inspection, products list regression, product detail regression, cabinet profile/payers/MA/documents/notifications regression). All 19/19 PASSED in 17s. Live inspection of Vita's two orders (7795, 7451) confirms item.image_url is now `uploads/products/LA277_LA277.jpg` and `uploads/products/GM002_1000232262.png` — exactly the expected single-prefix shape. No `uploads/uploads/` anywhere. Confirmed git shows only `from utils.image_helper import normalize_image_url` at line 21 of event_tool.py and no local def of normalize_image_url remains (grep verified all 6 call sites resolve to the global helper).
action_items: No code action needed. Double-prefix bug is fixed and verified end-to-end against live data.
critical_code_review_comments: Recommendation: add a module-level lint/CI rule (e.g. ruff F811 / pylint redefined-outer-name) to catch any future local shadowing of `normalize_image_url` (or any utils.* import). The bug was a textbook function-shadowing-import case that would have been caught statically.

All 6 call sites in event_tool.py (lines 538, 597, 606, 923, 1055, 1977) now uniformly use the single global helper — good. Consider extracting the legacy DB image-path handling into a single mapper layer instead of repeating `normalize_image_url(row[6])` etc., to reduce per-row defensive boilerplate.
seed_data_creation: None — used Vita's existing orders 7795 and 7451.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For any future change touching /app/backend/utils/image_helper.py or any image_url-emitting endpoint, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_image_url_double_prefix_fix.py (covers helper contract + 5 endpoints). Combine with iteration_4's two suites (test_cors_new_domain.py + test_cabinet_data_leak_fix.py) for a 36-case regression baseline. Vita has 2 orders (7795, 7451) with 1 item each, both with `uploads/products/...` shaped image_urls — useful for future visual checks.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: A local function `normalize_image_url(url)` was defined inside `get_my_order_by_id()` at /app/backend/routes/event_tool.py line ~1895, which shadowed the module-level `from utils.image_helper import normalize_image_url` (line 21). The local impl unconditionally prepended `/uploads/` regardless of input, so DB values like `uploads/products/STL9826_xxx.png` (which already include the prefix) became `/uploads/uploads/products/STL9826_xxx.png`. Frontend then concatenated `${REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/${url.replace(/^\/+/, '')}` → `https://farforevent.com.ua/uploads/uploads/products/...` → 404. REPRODUCTION (pre-fix): GET /api/event/orders/{order_id} on any owned order with `uploads/`-prefixed image_url in DB returned items[].image_url containing the double prefix. MITIGATION: Main agent already deleted the local function (lines 1895-1904 in pre-fix file). Verified: grep finds only the import + 6 call sites, no local def. Live data inspection confirms single-prefix output. PREVENTION: enable ruff F811 (redefined-outer-name) or pylint W0621 in CI to catch any future local shadowing of imported helpers.
